hypothesis: Sanity-check the remote environment end to end
notes: Smoke only; useful for environment validation not model comparison.

hypothesis: Establish the current-pod 8 GPU reference under repo-standard timing
train_changes: 8 GPU, 600s cap, full sp1024 train prefix, TRAIN_LOG_EVERY=50, VAL_LOSS_EVERY=200
notes: Use as the pod-specific reference; slower than 8xH100 leaderboard hardware.

hypothesis: Measure the cheapest eval-only gain before touching training
eval_changes: Sweep EVAL_STRIDE=512,256,128,64 on the same checkpoint
notes: Cheap win and required context for later comparisons.

hypothesis: Trade compression headroom for better model capacity
train_changes: Enable int6 export on large block weights and zstd compression
notes: Core enabler for the strongest public stack.

train_changes: Keep tok_emb.weight in fp16 during export
eval_changes: Compare standard eval and stride-64 eval
notes: Usually low-risk and synergistic with low-bit export.

hypothesis: Reinvest saved bytes into model capacity
train_changes: Increase MLP_MULT to 3 or hidden size near 1536
notes: Likely strongest architectural follow-up once bytes are free.

hypothesis: Reduce quantization damage by smoothing the weight distributions
train_changes: Try MATRIX_LR=0.02 SCALAR_LR=0.02 TIED_EMBED_LR=0.03 MUON_MOMENTUM=0.99 warmup from 0.92 WARMDOWN_ITERS=3000 GRAD_CLIP_NORM=1.0
eval_changes: Use the same eval recipe as the parent lane
notes: This is the optimizer pattern repeated across the top cluster.

hypothesis: Check whether modestly longer context improves quality without the full seq4096 throughput hit
train_changes: Set TRAIN_SEQ_LEN=2048 and rebalance batch tokens
eval_changes: Use stride-64 eval if the lane is competitive
notes: Good medium-risk lane if seq4096 is too expensive on this pod.

hypothesis: Test the long-context recipe used by several strong submissions
train_changes: Set TRAIN_SEQ_LEN=4096 and reduce TRAIN_BATCH_TOKENS accordingly
eval_changes: Match eval to the longer-context recipe
notes: Higher upside but slower steps on RTX Pro 6000 hardware.

hypothesis: See whether end-of-run averaging gives a free last-mile gain
train_changes: Add SWA near the end of warmdown or EMA every few steps
notes: Second-wave idea after the core quantization stack is stable.

hypothesis: Keep a small set of late K matrices at higher precision
train_changes: Leave selected late c_k weights in fp16 or grouped int8
notes: Targeted precision where the public lane saw measurable gains.

hypothesis: Use a cheap auxiliary objective during training only
train_changes: Add a lightweight MTP head excluded from the export artifact
notes: Promising if throughput overhead stays small on this box.

hypothesis: Test whether tiny pre-block token mixing adds cheap local context
train_changes: Add a learned previous-token blend before block 1
notes: Single strong public signal; worth trying after the main stack.

hypothesis: Explore larger tokenizers only after the main 1024-vocab lane is understood
train_changes: Try SP4096 or SP8192 with reduced depth or selective quant
eval_changes: Use sliding-window eval tuned for the tokenizer
notes: Interesting but not currently the strongest standard-track path.
